accurate.why: All claims align with the bill text, and contextual additions (SSA, Treasury's Do Not Pay working system, Section 205(r) of the Social Security Act) are verified as accurate.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; references to the Death Master File, SSA, and the Do Not Pay system are accurate real-world context, not inventions.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies the bill's purpose of improving SSA-Do Not Pay coordination to reduce improper payments to deceased people.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly explains that the bill amends Section 205(r) of the Social Security Act and modifies existing data-sharing and error-correction provisions.

exceptions_conditions.why: Captures key conditions: 'to the extent feasible,' privacy/security requirements, the cost-sharing agreement, and the clear-and-convincing-evidence standard.

effective_dates.why: Correctly states the amendments take effect December 27, 2026, matching the bill.

major_provisions_covered.why: Covers all operative provisions: data sharing to Do Not Pay, the cost-sharing agreement, the evidentiary standard for recording deaths, and the expanded error-notification requirement.

objective_tone.why: Neutral, descriptive tone with no advocacy or editorializing.

coherent.why: Well-organized and internally consistent, structured logically by provision.

concise.why: Compact with no padding or repetition.

correct_entities.why: Correctly names SSA, the Commissioner of Social Security, the Do Not Pay working system, and the Social Security Act; the Death Master File reference is accurate context.

correct_figures.why: The only figure stated, the December 27, 2026 effective date, is correct per the bill.
